title: Format Conversions Between Uniface and DBMS Connector
breadcrumbs: Format Conversions Between Uniface and DBMS Connector
text: Format Conversions Between Uniface and DBMS Connector To ensure that every Uniface packing code can be used with every supported DBMS, even if the underlying DBMS does not support that packing code, Uniface uses information from the individual database connectors. When first activated, a database connector presents to Uniface a full table of packing code mappings in which every packing code not supported by the DBMS is remapped to an alternative format. For example, if a DBMS does not support the date type, the connector typically redefines it as a character string. This causes Uniface to automatically convert a date field to a character string, and present it to the connector in that format. In this way, every supported DBMS gets only the data that it supports. It is this mechanism that is responsible for Uniface’s database independency. For the connector-specific mappings, see the connector documentation for your DBMS. Format Conversion When Storing Data When storing data, Uniface knows: the database interface definition for a field the DBMS connector being used the format that the connector requires for the database interface Uniface transparently corrects any field to the required format before passing it to the connector. CAUTION: Any numeric field should always have the appropriate syntax checks to ensure a user cannot enter values outside the domain range for that field, which would cause the database connector to store invalid data in the database. Format Conversion When Retrieving Data When retrieving data, the DBMS connector supplies the data to Uniface in the DBMS format. Just as with storing data, Uniface knows the database interface definition, the DBMS connector being used, and the format the data has when it comes from the DBMS connector. Uniface performs the appropriate conversion before passing a field to a component (or one of the Uniface 3GL service functions). This guarantees that the DBMS connector is always supplied with the data already converted to the required format, and that Uniface always knows how to convert the data for each field. Format Conversion When Using Data in 3GL Subroutines It is possible to use Uniface data in user-written 3GL modules. A 3GL module can be written in any high-level programming language like C or Pascal. It typically uses the Uniface 3GL subroutine UFGET() to get field data from a component, and can use UFPUT() to put it back after modification. UFGET() and UFPUT() cause data to be converted using the same packing code mappings as the database connector. For this reason, user-written 3GL code must take into account the specific database connector’s mapping rules. Using 3GL to access Uniface data is not recommended from a portability point of view. Related Links Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages.
